metadata.title: Why a Barrier Can Suddenly Turn Invisible
metadata.note: Clean rebuild. Scoped to coherent above-barrier scattering from an ideal rectangular barrier. Perfect transmission when qL=n*pi.

source_of_truth: beat_sheet.json — edit the sheet, not this file

beats.label: This is Liam, in for Bear. An ideal quantum barrier can reflect a wave at most energies, then become

beats.prompt: Assume the particle energy is above the rectangular barrier. The wave propagates in every region, but its wave number changes inside, so each boundary normally
beats.label: Assume the particle energy is above the rectangular barrier. The wave propagates in every region, bu

beats.prompt: How can two reflecting boundaries produce no reflection at all?
beats.label: How can two reflecting boundaries produce no reflection at all?

beats.prompt: At the first boundary, one reflected amplitude leaves immediately. Another part enters, reflects from the second boundary, and later returns through the first.
beats.label: At the first boundary, one reflected amplitude leaves immediately. Another part enters, reflects fro

beats.prompt: Those are only the first two paths. The wave can make repeated round trips inside the barrier, creating a whole geometric series of reflected amplitudes.
beats.label: Those are only the first two paths. The wave can make repeated round trips inside the barrier, creat

beats.prompt: Their relative phases depend on the internal wave number q and barrier width L. One traversal adds phase q L; one round trip adds two q L.
beats.label: Their relative phases depend on the internal wave number q and barrier width L. One traversal adds p

beats.prompt: When q L equals an integer times pi, the barrier contains an integer number of half wavelengths. The returning reflected contributions cancel the immediate refl
beats.label: When q L equals an integer times pi, the barrier contains an integer number of half wavelengths. The

beats.prompt: At that resonance, reflected probability falls to zero and transmission reaches one in the ideal one-dimensional model, despite the potential still being presen
beats.label: At that resonance, reflected probability falls to zero and transmission reaches one in the ideal one

beats.prompt: Move slightly away from the phase condition and cancellation fails. Reflection returns. A scan of energy shows narrow transmission peaks at successive resonance
beats.label: Move slightly away from the phase condition and cancellation fails. Reflection returns. A scan of en

beats.prompt: Changing the width shifts the resonances because the product q L controls the phase. Double L, and the matching energies rearrange.
beats.label: Changing the width shifts the resonances because the product q L controls the phase. Double L, and t

beats.label: This is a coherent above-barrier effect for an ideal rectangular barrier. Absorption, disorder, deph

beats.prompt: The barrier does not vanish. Its reflected paths erase one another. At the right internal phase, an obstacle becomes transparent by interference.
